purpose: Pre-commit hook driver (block-narrow mode per AI_UNDERSTANDING_FORMAT.md §9). Self-contained Node script — copied into a target repo's .git/hooks/ at install time. Reads tracked_globs from _meta.json; for each staged source matched, blocks if the .aiu.json sidecar is missing in the staged tree or its sha1 differs from sha1 of the staged source. Blocks orphan sidecar deletions.
invariants: No _meta.json = no contract = exit 0 (no-op). The hook only enforces what the project has opted into.
sha1 comparison uses STAGED content for both source and sidecar — unstaged working-tree changes are intentionally ignored.
Self-contained: depends only on Node builtins. The glob matcher is a copy of understanding.js#globToRegex (intentional duplication — hook must not require the extension to be installed).
Exit 0 = pass; exit 1 = block. Non-zero unexpected-error path also exits 1 with diagnostic to stderr.
gotchas: Override is `git commit --no-verify` — printed in the failure message every time so the user always sees the escape hatch.
gitListStaged returns []  on any git error (e.g. partial index corruption) — failure-of-list-staged silently passes the commit.
The driver is COPIED into .git/hooks/aiu-precommit.js at install — editing src/cli/aiu-precommit.js in the extension repo does not update already-installed hooks until the user reinstalls.
